message.content:
You are an independent fix-confirmation reviewer (reviewer A) for a production-hardening pass on the hc-assay repo at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay. Be adversarial and skeptical — your job is to find any way the fix is wrong, incomplete, or its regression test fails to discriminate.

FINDING J-004 (MED):
MLflow TOCTOU: _experiment_id does check-then-create; two concurrent first-time start_run calls (8-thread reasoning pool / multiple study processes on one shared local store) both see None and both create_experiment; MLflow rejects the loser with RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS, the exception propagates out of start_run leaving run_id=None and silently dropping ALL metrics + the provenance artifact. Fix: wrap create_experiment; on any exception re-fetch get_experiment_by_name and return it if now present, else re-raise. In src/assay_engine/observability/tracking.py. Test: test_experiment_id_survives_concurrent_create_race.

The complete code diff for all pass-7 fixes is at /tmp/pass7_code.diff (read it). The full files are under /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay. You may run the test suite (cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python -m pytest -q <path>) — NOTE: use "python -m pytest", not bare "pytest", or the tests/ package won't import.

Judge ONLY finding J-004. Decide:
- appropriate: does the fix address the real root cause with a sound approach (not a band-aid, not the wrong layer)?
- complete: is there any remaining gap, AND does the regression test actually FAIL if the fix is reverted (i.e. does it discriminate)? If you can, reason about or test revert-discrimination.
Return CONFIRMED only if both are true. Return CONCERN if appropriate but with a real residual gap. Return REJECTED if the fix is wrong or the test is non-discriminating. List concrete concerns.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll review finding J-004. Let me start by reading the diff and the relevant code.

content.content:
1	"""Experiment tracking — a local, on-box run/metric/artifact store (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's MLflow setup. The backend is a local store
4	(default: a SQLite file under the working directory); a remote tracking server is rejected by
5	the local-URI guard. MLflow is optional (the ``observability`` extra) and imported lazily, so
6	this module loads without it.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import os
12	from pathlib import Path
13	from typing import Any, Mapping, Protocol
14	
15	from assay_engine._local import require_local_uri
16	
17	_DEFAULT_URI = "sqlite:///assay_mlflow.db"
18	EXPERIMENT_NAME = os.environ.get("ASSAY_EXPERIMENT", "assay")
19	
20	
21	def get_tracking_uri() -> str:
22	    """Resolve the tracking URI (env override, else local SQLite), enforced local."""
23	    uri = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACKING_URI", _DEFAULT_URI)
24	    # Resolve a relative sqlite path to an absolute file so runs land deterministically.
25	    prefix = "sqlite:///"
26	    if uri.startswith(prefix):
27	        rel = uri[len(prefix) :]
28	        if not os.path.isabs(rel):
29	            uri = prefix + str((Path.cwd() / rel).resolve())
30	    return require_local_uri(uri, what="experiment tracking")
31	
32	
33	# Structural Protocol only — adapter/seam validation is behavior-based, not isinstance (#148).
34	class ExperimentTracker(Protocol):
35	    def start_run(self, name: str, params: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str: ...
36	    def log_metric(self, run_id: str, key: str, value: float) -> None: ...
37	    def log_artifact(self, run_id: str, path: str) -> None: ...
38	    def end_run(self, run_id: str, status: str = "FINISHED") -> None: ...
39	
40	
41	class MlflowExperimentTracker:
42	    """A local-store experiment tracker backed by MLflow (lazy import).
43	
44	    Construction validates the tracking URI is local and selects the experiment; it does not
45	    import MLflow until a run is started, so the module is importable without the extra.
46	    """
47	
48	    def __init__(self, experiment: str = EXPERIMENT_NAME) -> None:
49	        self._experiment = experiment
50	        self._uri = get_tracking_uri()  # validates local before anything else
51	
52	    def _client(self) -> Any:
53	        """An MlflowClient bound to the local store. Uses the client API (keyed by run_id)
54	        throughout, so no run ever sits on the global active-run stack — start_run followed by
55	        log_metric cannot collide (audit issue #O1)."""
56	        try:
57	            from mlflow.tracking import MlflowClient
58	        except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
59	            raise RuntimeError(
60	                "experiment tracking requires the 'observability' extra (mlflow) — not installed"
61	            ) from exc
62	        return MlflowClient(tracking_uri=self._uri)
63	
64	    def _experiment_id(self, client: Any) -> str:
65	        exp = client.get_experiment_by_name(self._experiment)
66	        if exp is not None:
67	            return str(exp.experiment_id)
68	        # check-then-create is a TOCTOU (#J-004): two concurrent first-time start_run calls (the
69	        # 8-thread reasoning pool, or multiple study processes on one shared local store) can both
70	        # see None and both create_experiment; MLflow rejects the loser with RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS
71	        # and the exception would propagate out of start_run, leaving run_id=None and silently
72	        # dropping ALL metrics + the provenance artifact for the whole study. On a create race,
73	        # re-fetch the now-existing experiment instead of failing.
74	        try:
75	            return str(client.create_experiment(self._experiment))
76	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - normalize the concurrent-create race to a re-fetch
77	            exp = client.get_experiment_by_name(self._experiment)
78	            if exp is not None:
79	                return str(exp.experiment_id)
80	            raise
81	
82	    def start_run(self, name: str, params: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str:
83	        client = self._client()
84	        run = client.create_run(self._experiment_id(client), run_name=name)
85	        run_id = str(run.info.run_id)
86	        # The run now exists in the store. If param-logging fails partway (network blip, server
87	        # error), the run would otherwise be orphaned permanently in RUNNING state — phantom runs
88	        # accumulate over time (pass 3, #F-023). Terminate it FAILED on any param-logging error
89	        # before re-raising, so a created run is never left dangling.
90	        try:
91	            for k, v in dict(params).items():
92	                client.log_param(run_id, k, v)
93	        except Exception:
94	            try:
95	                client.set_terminated(run_id, status="FAILED")
96	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001,S110 - best-effort cleanup; original error re-raised
97	                pass
98	            raise
99	        return run_id
100	
101	    def log_metric(self, run_id: str, key: str, value: float) -> None:
102	        self._client().log_metric(run_id, key, value)
103	
104	    def log_artifact(self, run_id: str, path: str) -> None:
105	        self._client().log_artifact(run_id, path)
106	
107	    def end_run(self, run_id: str, status: str = "FINISHED") -> None:
108	        # MLflow run statuses: FINISHED / FAILED / KILLED. Recording FAILED makes a failed run
109	        # distinguishable from a successful one in the store (#110).
110	        self._client().set_terminated(run_id, status=status)
111

diff --git a/.github/workflows/ci.yml b/.github/workflows/ci.yml
index ed0873a..9e1e25c 100644
--- a/.github/workflows/ci.yml
+++ b/.github/workflows/ci.yml
@@ -32,7 +32,8 @@ jobs:
           # core lock carries only the `dev` extra (no backends), preserving the ADR-0006 guard.
           python -m pip install --upgrade pip
           pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-core.lock
-          pip install --no-deps -e .
+          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-build.lock  # hashed build backend (#J-003)
+          pip install --no-build-isolation --no-deps -e .
       - name: Lint (ruff)
         run: ruff check src tests
       - name: Format check (ruff)
@@ -77,7 +78,8 @@ jobs:
           # release within a pyproject range could run during the real-backend tests (#F-004).
           python -m pip install --upgrade pip
           pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.lock
-          pip install --no-deps -e .
+          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-build.lock  # hashed build backend (#J-003)
+          pip install --no-build-isolation --no-deps -e .
       - name: Type-check against real backend types (mypy)
         run: mypy
       - name: Verify service containers are reachable (no silent skip)
@@ -136,6 +138,12 @@ jobs:
           uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock
           diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements-core.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock) \
             || { echo "::error::requirements-core.lock is stale — run: uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements-core.lock"; exit 1; }
+          # the build-backend lockfile (hatchling etc.) backs the hash-pinned --no-build-isolation
+          # editable install (#J-003) and must stay in sync.
+          printf 'hatchling\neditables\n' > /tmp/build-reqs.in
+          uv pip compile /tmp/build-reqs.in --universal --generate-hashes -o /tmp/requirements-build.regen.lock
+          diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements-build.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements-build.regen.lock) \
+            || { echo "::error::requirements-build.lock is stale — run: uv pip compile <(printf 'hatchling\\neditables\\n') --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements-build.lock"; exit 1; }
       - name: pip-audit (reproducible known-vulnerability scan)
         run: |
           set -euo pipefail
@@ -188,9 +196,10 @@ jobs:
           # live `pip install -e .[all]` resolution that could pull different versions than the
           # lockfile pins (#F-013). Advisory monitoring of the rest is handled by Dependabot.
           pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.lock
-          pip install --no-deps -e .
+          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-build.lock  # hashed build backend (#J-003)
+          pip install --no-build-isolation --no-deps -e .
           python -m pip install pip-licenses==5.5.5  # pinned for reproducible license output (#G-022)
-          pip-licenses --format=json --with-system > licenses.json
+          pip-licenses --format=json > licenses.json  # scan the dep set, not system tooling (#J-006)
           python scripts/license_gate.py licenses.json
       - name: Upload license report
         if: always()
diff --git a/requirements-build.lock b/requirements-build.lock
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..4b1395d
--- /dev/null
+++ b/requirements-build.lock
@@ -0,0 +1,26 @@
+# This file was autogenerated by uv via the following command:
+#    uv pip compile /tmp/build-reqs.in --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements-build.lock
+editables==0.6 \
+    --hash=sha256:1163834902381c4613787951c5914800fdf155ae08848a373b8ea5006780977c \
+    --hash=sha256:d70e4698078a1d033e7786d9c64e5be070d058a67c21417024d38a58ac20aa43
+    # via -r /tmp/build-reqs.in
+hatchling==1.30.1 \
+    --hash=sha256:161eacafb3c6f91526e92116d21426369f2c36e98c36a864f11a96345ad4ee31 \
+    --hash=sha256:eee4fd45357f72ebb3d7a42e5d72cfb5e29ed426d79e8836288926c4258d5f2e
+    # via -r /tmp/build-reqs.in
+packaging==26.2 \
+    --hash=sha256:5fc45236b9446107ff2415ce77c807cee2862cb6fac22b8a73826d0693b0980e \
+    --hash=sha256:ff452ff5a3e828ce110190feff1178bb1f2ea2281fa2075aadb987c2fb221661
+    # via hatchling
+pathspec==1.1.1 \
+    --hash=sha256:17db5ecd524104a120e173814c90367a96a98d07c45b2e10c2f3919fff91bf5a \
+    --hash=sha256:a00ce642f577bf7f473932318056212bc4f8bfdf53128c78bbd5af0b9b20b189
+    # via hatchling
+pluggy==1.6.0 \
+    --hash=sha256:7dcc130b76258d33b90f61b658791dede3486c3e6bfb003ee5c9bfb396dd22f3 \
+    --hash=sha256:e920276dd6813095e9377c0bc5566d94c932c33b27a3e3945d8389c374dd4746
+    # via hatchling
+trove-classifiers==2026.6.1.19 \
+    --hash=sha256:ab4c4ec93cc4a4e7815fa759906e05e6bb3f2fbd92ea0f897288c6a43efd15b3 \
+    --hash=sha256:c5132b4b61a829d11cfbd2d72e97f20a45ed6edb95e45c5efdeb5e00836b2745
+    # via hatchling
diff --git a/scripts/capture_transcripts.py b/scripts/capture_transcripts.py
index 465013e..fb2ff89 100755
--- a/scripts/capture_transcripts.py
+++ b/scripts/capture_transcripts.py
@@ -73,7 +73,52 @@ def _username_pattern() -> re.Pattern[str] | None:
     return re.compile(re.escape(name))
 
 
+def _display_name_patterns() -> list[re.Pattern[str]]:
+    """Redactions for the operator's full DISPLAY NAME (#J-002).
+
+    The username/email rules leave the real name (e.g. a git ``user.name`` like "[REDACTED]")
+    in cleartext — distinct operator PII that appears in git author strings, ``pyproject``
+    ``authors=``, and prose, often right beside an already-redacted email. Derive candidate names
+    from ``git config user.name`` and the ``ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES`` env (comma-separated), redact each
+    (and, for a multi-word name, its whitespace-flexible form). Length-guarded to avoid
+    over-redacting common words.
+    """
+    names: list[str] = []
+    extra = os.environ.get("ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES", "")
+    names.extend(n.strip() for n in extra.split(",") if n.strip())
+    # Derive the operator name from SEVERAL sources — any one may be empty in a given context
+    # (#J-002 follow-up): on this node local `git config user.name` is unset yet commits still
+    # carry an author via global config / env, so a single-source lookup scrubbed nothing. Cover
+    # the env author vars, git config, AND the actual last-commit author.
+    for env_name in ("GIT_AUTHOR_NAME", "GIT_COMMITTER_NAME"):
+        v = os.environ.get(env_name, "").strip()
+        if v:
+            names.append(v)
+    try:
+        import subprocess
+
+        for cmd in (["git", "config", "user.name"], ["git", "log", "-1", "--format=%an"]):
+            out = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5)
+            if out.returncode == 0 and out.stdout.strip():
+                names.append(out.stdout.strip())
+    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - git absent/misconfigured must never break capture
+        pass
+    pats: list[re.Pattern[str]] = []
+    seen: set[str] = set()
+    for n in names:
+        if len(n) < 4 or n in seen:
+            continue  # too short → risks over-redacting common words; or already added
+        seen.add(n)
+        # Flexible inter-token whitespace (e.g. "[REDACTED]"). NO leading \b: the name often
+        # appears glued to a JSON-escaped newline ("...---\\n[REDACTED]") where \b doesn't fire
+        # because the escape's 'n' abuts the name (#J-002 follow-up). A trailing \b still prevents
+        # matching inside a longer word (e.g. "Joneses"); the >=4-char guard bounds over-redaction.
+        pats.append(re.compile(r"\s+".join(re.escape(t) for t in n.split()) + r"\b"))
+    return pats
+
+
 _USERNAME_RE = _username_pattern()
+_DISPLAY_NAME_RES = _display_name_patterns()
 
 
 def _scrub(text: str) -> str:
@@ -88,6 +133,10 @@ def _scrub(text: str) -> str:
     # don't re-expose it. Done last so it also catches it inside project-dir slugs / git authors.
     if _USERNAME_RE is not None:
         text = _USERNAME_RE.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
+    # Redact the operator's full display name too (#J-002) — distinct PII the username/email rules
+    # leave in cleartext (git author strings, pyproject authors=, prose).
+    for pat in _DISPLAY_NAME_RES:
+        text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
     return text
 
 
@@ -104,13 +153,18 @@ def _copy_scrubbed(srcf: Path, dstf: Path) -> dict | None:
         # Scrub EVERY text file, not only .jsonl (#CV-O-1): the session subtree also holds .json
         # workflow/subagent transcripts that carry the same operator PII + credential-shaped
         # secrets — the old suffix==".jsonl" gate copied those RAW, bypassing redaction entirely.
-        # Decode strictly to classify: text → scrub; genuinely binary (rare here) → copy verbatim.
-        try:
-            text = raw.decode("utf-8")
-        except UnicodeDecodeError:
-            data = raw  # binary blob — nothing to scrub
+        #
+        # FAIL CLOSED on redaction (#J-001): classify binary by an actual binary SIGNAL (a NUL
+        # byte), NOT by UTF-8 validity. A transcript routinely embeds arbitrary subprocess stdout
+        # (latin-1 dumps, truncated multibyte) inside JSONL strings; gating on strict decode meant
+        # a single stray byte routed the WHOLE file to the verbatim branch and re-leaked it (the
+        # CV-O-1 fix had turned an always-redact path into a fail-open one). Decode with
+        # errors="replace" so redaction ALWAYS runs on text; only a genuinely binary blob (NUL
+        # present) is copied verbatim.
+        if b"\x00" in raw:
+            data = raw  # genuinely binary — nothing to scrub
         else:
-            data = _scrub(text).encode("utf-8")
+            data = _scrub(raw.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")).encode("utf-8")
         if dstf.exists() and dstf.read_bytes() == data:
             pass  # unchanged — still report it in the manifest
         else:
diff --git a/src/assay_engine/observability/tracking.py b/src/assay_engine/observability/tracking.py
index f00cd07..01fdff2 100644
--- a/src/assay_engine/observability/tracking.py
+++ b/src/assay_engine/observability/tracking.py
@@ -65,7 +65,19 @@ class MlflowExperimentTracker:
         exp = client.get_experiment_by_name(self._experiment)
         if exp is not None:
             return str(exp.experiment_id)
-        return str(client.create_experiment(self._experiment))
+        # check-then-create is a TOCTOU (#J-004): two concurrent first-time start_run calls (the
+        # 8-thread reasoning pool, or multiple study processes on one shared local store) can both
+        # see None and both create_experiment; MLflow rejects the loser with RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS
+        # and the exception would propagate out of start_run, leaving run_id=None and silently
+        # dropping ALL metrics + the provenance artifact for the whole study. On a create race,
+        # re-fetch the now-existing experiment instead of failing.
+        try:
+            return str(client.create_experiment(self._experiment))
+        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - normalize the concurrent-create race to a re-fetch
+            exp = client.get_experiment_by_name(self._experiment)
+            if exp is not None:
+                return str(exp.experiment_id)
+            raise
 
     def start_run(self, name: str, params: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str:
         client = self._client()
diff --git a/tests/integration/test_reasoning_drain.py b/tests/integration/test_reasoning_drain.py
index 79964bf..106212f 100644
--- a/tests/integration/test_reasoning_drain.py
+++ b/tests/integration/test_reasoning_drain.py
@@ -140,10 +140,20 @@ def test_t3_hung_subprocess_is_cancelled_not_leaked(fake_sdk, monkeypatch):
     assert _t.monotonic() - t0 < 5  # cancelled promptly near the 0.3s inner timeout, not hung
 
 
+def _assert_transient(excinfo):
+    # #J-005: RateLimitError and PermanentReasoningError both SUBCLASS ReasoningError, so
+    # `pytest.raises(ReasoningError)` does NOT discriminate a transient mapping from a rate-limit
+    # or permanent one. Assert the exception is the transient class itself, not a decisive subclass.
+    assert not isinstance(excinfo.value, (RateLimitError, PermanentReasoningError)), (
+        f"expected a transient ReasoningError, got {type(excinfo.value).__name__}"
+    )
+
+
 def test_t3_empty_reply_raises_transient(fake_sdk):
     fake_sdk.append(_AssistantMessage([]))  # no text blocks -> empty
-    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError):
+    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError) as ei:
         _run()
+    _assert_transient(ei)
 
 
 def test_t3_rate_limit_event_rejected_maps_to_ratelimit(fake_sdk):
@@ -167,8 +177,9 @@ def test_t3_auth_error_maps_to_permanent(fake_sdk):
 
 def test_t3_generic_error_maps_to_transient(fake_sdk):
     fake_sdk.append(_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=500, errors=["boom"]))
-    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError):
+    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError) as ei:
         _run()

diff --git a/tests/test_observability.py b/tests/test_observability.py
index 84831f6..64b6aee 100644
--- a/tests/test_observability.py
+++ b/tests/test_observability.py

     assert any("setup failed" in r.message for r in caplog.records)
 
 
+def test_experiment_id_survives_concurrent_create_race(monkeypatch, tmp_path):
+    # #J-004: check-then-create is a TOCTOU — on the first-time race, create_experiment raises
+    # RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS for the loser. _experiment_id must re-fetch the now-existing
+    # experiment instead of propagating (which would leave run_id=None and drop all metrics).
+    monkeypatch.setenv("ASSAY_TRACKING_URI", f"sqlite:///{tmp_path / 'x.db'}")
+
+    class _Exp:
+        experiment_id = "exp-7"
+
+    class RacingClient:
+        def __init__(self):
+            self.calls = 0
+
+        def get_experiment_by_name(self, name):
+            # first call (the check) sees nothing; after the failed create, the winner's exists
+            self.calls += 1
+            return None if self.calls == 1 else _Exp()
+
+        def create_experiment(self, name):
+            raise RuntimeError("RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS: another writer created it")
+
+    t = MlflowExperimentTracker()
+    client = RacingClient()
+    assert t._experiment_id(client) == "exp-7"  # re-fetched, not raised
+
+
 def test_start_run_terminates_run_when_param_logging_fails(monkeypatch, tmp_path):
     # #F-023: if create_run succeeds but log_param fails, the run must be terminated FAILED, not
     # left orphaned in RUNNING state. Mock the MLflow client to exercise the failure path without
diff --git a/tests/test_scripts.py b/tests/test_scripts.py
index 510b00f..b66eceb 100644
--- a/tests/test_scripts.py
+++ b/tests/test_scripts.py
@@ -123,6 +123,39 @@ def test_copy_scrubbed_copies_binary_verbatim(tmp_path):
     assert dst.read_bytes() == b"\xff\xfe\x00\x01binary"
 
 
+def test_scrub_fails_closed_on_invalid_utf8_bytes():
+    # #J-001: a stray non-UTF-8 byte must NOT disable redaction for the whole file. The scrubber
+    # decodes errors='replace' and classifies binary by a NUL byte, so a secret next to an invalid
+    # byte is still scrubbed (the pass-6 strict-decode fix had made this fail OPEN).
+    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_failclosed")
+    raw = b'{"out":"[REDACTED] caf\xe9 /Users/[REDACTED]/x"}'  # invalid \xe9
+    text = raw.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
+    out = cap._scrub(text)
+    assert "[REDACTED]" not in out  # secret scrubbed despite the bad byte
+    assert "somebody" not in out
+
+
+def test_copy_scrubbed_scrubs_file_with_invalid_utf8(tmp_path):
+    # #J-001 end-to-end: a .jsonl with an invalid byte is still scrubbed on copy, not copied raw.
+    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_fc2")
+    src = tmp_path / "t.jsonl"
+    src.write_bytes(b'{"k":"[REDACTED] \x80 end"}')  # invalid \x80
+    dst = tmp_path / "out" / "t.jsonl"
+    cap._copy_scrubbed(src, dst)
+    out = dst.read_bytes()
+    assert b"abcdefghijklmnopqrstuv" not in out and b"[REDACTED]" in out
+
+
+def test_scrub_redacts_operator_display_name(monkeypatch):
+    # #J-002: the operator's full display name (from ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES / git user.name) is redacted,
+    # not just the email — distinct PII that leaked beside redacted emails.
+    monkeypatch.setenv("ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES", "Ada Lovelace")
+    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_name")
+    out = cap._scrub("Author: Ada Lovelace <[REDACTED]>; also Ada   Lovelace in prose")
+    assert "Ada Lovelace" not in out and "Ada   Lovelace" not in out  # flexible whitespace too
+    assert "[REDACTED]" not in out  # email still scrubbed
+
+
 def test_example_uses_no_hardcoded_hmac_secret():
     # #F-048: the example must not carry a shippable, publicly-known HMAC secret a user could copy
     # into production. It derives a fresh random secret per run instead.

+def test_ci_pins_build_backend_and_disables_live_build_isolation():
+    # #J-003: the editable install must use the hashed build backend (requirements-build.lock) with
+    # --no-build-isolation, so hatchling isn't fetched live/un-hashed from PyPI (an unscanned,
+    # non-reproducible build-time code-exec window).
+    text = _ci_text()
+    assert "--no-build-isolation --no-deps -e ." in text, "build isolation still live (#J-003)"
+    assert "requirements-build.lock" in text
+    assert (_ROOT / "requirements-build.lock").exists()
+    build = (_ROOT / "requirements-build.lock").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
+    assert "hatchling==" in build and "--hash=sha256:" in build
+    assert "pip install --no-deps -e .\n" not in text  # the old live-isolation install is gone
+
+
+def test_ci_license_gate_scopes_to_dependency_set():
+    # #J-006: the license gate must scan the dependency set, not entire site-packages
+    # (--with-system pulls system tooling that can spuriously fail or mask the gate).
+    text = _ci_text()
+    assert "pip-licenses --format=json --with-system" not in text, (
+        "license gate still --with-system"
+    )
+
+
 def test_github_actions_are_sha_pinned():

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && git diff --stat src/assay_engine/observability/tracking.py && python -m pytest -q tests/test_observability.py 2>&1 | tail -5
input.description: Confirm restore clean and full obs suite passes

content.text:
Restore is clean (no diff) and the full observability suite passes.

Assessment of J-004:

Root cause: `_experiment_id` did unguarded check-then-create. The fix wraps `create_experiment` and on any exception re-fetches `get_experiment_by_name`, returning the now-existing experiment, else re-raises. This is the correct layer (the exact TOCTOU site) and the standard idiom for this race.

Discrimination confirmed: test passes with fix, fails (RuntimeError propagates) when reverted.

A few observations, none rising to a rejection:
- The `except Exception` is broad: a genuinely unrelated `create_experiment` failure (e.g. permission/store error) that also happens to leave the experiment retrievable would be swallowed. In practice for this race the re-fetch only succeeds if a concurrent writer actually created it, and any other failure re-raises, so behavior is acceptable. The breadth is deliberately commented.
- The test simulates the race with a stub (`create_experiment` always raises, second `get` returns the experiment) rather than real concurrency. This is appropriate for a deterministic unit test and exercises the exact recovery branch; it cannot prove real MLflow raises a catchable exception, but MLflow's `RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS` is raised as `MlflowException` (a normal Exception), so the broad catch covers it.
- Residual non-gap: if both writers race and neither re-fetch sees the experiment (extremely unlikely given the winner committed before raising on the loser), it correctly re-raises rather than masking — fail-loud, acceptable.

No remaining gap that affects correctness of the fix or the test's discrimination.

input.rationale: The fix in /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/observability/tracking.py (_experiment_id, lines 64-80) addresses the exact TOCTOU root cause: it wraps create_experiment and, on any exception, re-fetches get_experiment_by_name, returning the now-existing experiment and only re-raising if it is still absent. This is the standard idiom applied at the correct layer (the precise check-then-create site), not a band-aid. The regression test test_experiment_id_survives_concurrent_create_race discriminates: it passes with the fix and FAILS (the RuntimeError propagates) when I reverted to the plain create_experiment, which I verified by editing the file, rerunning, and restoring (git diff clean afterward). The full observability suite passes (25 passed, 2 skipped). The call path is consistent — start_run passes the same client into _experiment_id, so the recovery applies on the real failure path.
concerns: except Exception is intentionally broad: an unrelated create_experiment failure that nonetheless leaves the experiment retrievable would be swallowed; mitigated because re-fetch only succeeds when a concurrent writer actually created it and all other failures re-raise.

The test stubs the race deterministically rather than exercising real concurrency; it cannot independently prove MLflow raises a catchable Exception, though MLflow's RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS is an MlflowException covered by the broad catch.

content.text: CONFIRMED. J-004 fix is sound and the regression test discriminates (verified by revert). No action needed on the task list from my side as a reviewer subagent.
